evals.prompt: Run the full pre-market analysis across SPY, QQQ, IWM, XLE, XLF, GLD, TLT, and SMH. VIX is at 27. Overnight futures were down 0.4% but recovered. There was a geopolitical ceasefire rumor that was then denied, causing whipsaw in futures. I have existing Bull Put spreads on SPY, QQQ, IWM, and GLD. Give me today's trading plan.
evals.expected_output: Complete pre-market analysis including news search, 4 analyst perspectives, chief strategist synthesis, and specific trade recommendations with strike prices and Kelly sizing
expectations: Executes WebSearch for latest market news before analysis
Includes credibility assessment of the ceasefire rumor (flags as noise if insufficient sources)
Provides perspectives from all 4 analysts (macro, sector, quant, event)
Produces multi-scenario ETF mispricing table with equilibrium prices and gap_pct
Includes scenario probability update suggestions from at least 2 analysts
Chief strategist includes disagreement scan and explicit reasoning for analyst weighting
Trade recommendations include specific ETF, strike guidance, DTE, and Kelly sizing
Includes VIX-27 risk management note (0.7x Kelly adjustment)
At least one analyst provides Devil's Advocate or 'If I'm Wrong' section
Executive summary in 5-line format at the top of the report
Output language matches user's language or defaults to English

evals.prompt: Post-market review: SPY dropped 1.8% today, VIX surged from 27 to 31, GLD rallied 2%, XLE fell 3%. My SPY Bull Put spread is near its stop-loss. Give me the post-market analysis and tomorrow's action plan.
evals.expected_output: Post-market analysis with P&L assessment, position management recommendations, overnight risk assessment, and tomorrow's preparation
expectations: Searches for today's market close reasons and catalysts
Assesses the SPY Bull Put spread status relative to stop-loss
Provides a clear close/hold/roll recommendation with specific reasoning
Analyzes GLD rally as potential new opportunity
Adjusts scenario weights for high-VIX environment (geopolitical/recession scenarios up)
Includes 'If I Was Wrong' postmortem on morning predictions
Tomorrow's action plan includes specific time-based triggers and conditions
Chief strategist explains disagreements between analysts' views
Output format: 5-line overnight brief + detailed sections

evals.prompt: Intraday 11:00 AM: A Fed official just made an unexpected dovish comment. SPY futures jumped 0.8% in 5 minutes. VIX dropped from 29 to 26. Is this a real opportunity or market noise? What should I do?
evals.expected_output: Rapid signal assessment with noise/signal classification, opportunity evaluation, and specific actionable recommendation
expectations: Uses WebSearch to verify the Fed official's comment authenticity
Classifies as signal or noise based on multi-source confirmation
Assesses whether 5-minute 0.8% move is an overreaction (questions time frame)
Evaluates VIX drop impact on credit spread strategy
If signal: provides specific credit spread recommendation with timing
Considers scenario probability impact (dovish Fed → policy pivot scenario higher)
Includes counter-scenario: 'if re-interpreted as hawkish'
Respects intraday 80-line report length constraint
